author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Blues on the Beach
summary: Wednesday may be hump day in the workaday world, but July Wednesdays on Block Island mean winding down your day with live blues along the beach. Bring a beach chair or blanket and dance the sand off (or simply chill if you’re so inclined) to favorite Block Island and New England musicians.
content:
Wednesday may be hump day in the workaday world, but July Wednesdays on Block Island mean winding down your day with live blues along the beach. Bring a beach chair or blanket and dance the sand off (or simply chill if you’re so inclined) to favorite Block Island and New England musicians. 

The music starts right around 6 pm—after the lifeguards have left their posts, early enough for young kids to enjoy, and early enough to head out for dinner after. In case of rain, the location changes from beachside to Captain Nick’s at 34 Ocean Avenue.  

Oh, and more good news. Like Block Island sunshine and gentle breezes, Blues on the Block is absolutely free.

meta.title: Blues on the Block
meta.description: The Blues on the Block dates are July 11, July 25, August 1 and August 15.

Wednesday may be hump day in the workaday world, but July Wednesdays on Block Island mean winding down your day with live blues along the beach. Bring a beach chair or blanket and dance the sand off (or simply chill if you’re so inclined) to favorite Block Island and New England musicians. 

The music starts right around 6 pm — after the lifeguards have left their posts, early enough for young kids to enjoy, and early enough to head out for dinner after. In case of rain, the location changes from beachside to Captain Nick’s at 34 Ocean Avenue.

Just another reason to love this island.

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2025 Halloween Dinner at Winfield's
summary: Winfield's will reopen just one night for a prefix Halloween Dinner.
content:
Winfield's will reopen just one night for a prefix Halloween Dinner.

 

Join us for a spellbinding evening of fine food, wickedly good wine, and Halloween magic. We’ve received some thoughtful feedback that a few guests would like to enjoy the dinner without wine pairings. To make the evening more inclusive, we’re pleased to offer two options:

  •  5-Course Dinner with Wine Pairings – $150
  •  5-Course Dinner without Wine Pairings – $100

Our bar will also be open should you wish to sip something else.

Click the link below to reserve your seat at the table — reservations are required before this hauntingly good evening disappears into the night!


A $25 deposit per guest is required to reserve your seat via Tock https://www.exploretock.com/winfields-block-island-new-shoreham/event/575229.  This amount will be applied to your final bill at the event.

Spots are limited — reserve yours now and treat yourself to an evening of flavor & fun!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Full Moon Rise Walk
summary: Join us for an evening stroll to watch the full moon rise over the ocean. Meet at Mansion Beach lower parking lot.
content:
Join us for an evening stroll to watch the full moon rise over the ocean. Meet at Mansion Beach lower parking lot.

Advance sign-up is recommended, not required: email Kim.Gaffett@tnc.org

author: Austin Crawford
title: Prohibition Party at Yellow Kittens
summary: Prohibition Party at Yellow Kittens Thursday, August 14 at 6:30pm for:
content:
Prohibition Party at Yellow Kittens Thursday, August 14 at 6:30pm for:

  • ﻿﻿A screening of The Real McCoy documentary with an introduction by filmmaker and distiller Bailey Pryor.
  • ﻿﻿Learning about the pioneer Rum Runner of the prohibition era, well-known for delivering undiluted alcohol.
  • ﻿﻿Live music by The Sea Muensters
  • ﻿﻿Rum Tastings & Giveaways!

 

$10 donation to benefit the Block Island Historical Society

author: Austin Crawford
title: Tunes by the Dunes
summary: Join in the fun of the first-ever jam session at the Fred Benson Block Island Town Beach!  All instruments and voices are welcome to join in the strumming, singing, and/or listening to the live music!  We will work from lyrics and chords projected on a screen. So, bring your enthusiasm and beach chair for this evening of fun.  We have an introduction to basic Uke chords at 6:30  P.M and then the jam session for all instruments and singers begins at 7:00 P.M and ends at 8:00 P.M.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Lost to the Sea: Cemetery Tour Block Island Historical Society
summary: Janet Dionis, Education Director of the Block Island Historical Society, will highlight the headstones and stories of those claimed by the sea.
content:
Janet Dionis, Education Director of the Block Island Historical Society, will highlight the headstones and stories of those claimed by the sea. 

Meet at the cemetery gate at 6:45.

In the event of heavy rain, the tour will be moved indoors to the Historical Society for a slide show and discussion. Please check here for updates, and leave us your phone number in case of cancellation.

Tickets are $25

 

https://www.blockislandhistorical.org/
